data.task:
READ-ONLY research atom (deep-read; make NO edits, commit nothing). Goal: produce the primary evidence needed to decide the lightest fix for each of the four run_181 orchestration-loop failure modes, so the founder can ratify each conclusion. Weigh F5 throughout: do NOT propose any docs/process-policing checker; where a check is warranted it must guard an agent->reality boundary, not process conformance.

Return a structured markdown report with one section per failure mode. For EACH mode give: (a) the exact command(s) you ran and their raw output, (b) the file+line that currently owns the relevant standard/prompt/Play/test, and (c) a one-paragraph judgment of the lightest fix and whether it is (a) prompt/standard text, (b) an agent->reality boundary check, or (c) an accepted no-op. Do not write changes; draft proposed text inline in the report only.

Mode 1 (stale ticket site list -> defect-class miss): Identify the leak defect class from run_181 (the resource/handle leak fixed across record.ts / runner.ts / daemon/src/launcher.ts). Run the single live grep that re-derives the COMPLETE site set for that defect class across the current tree, and show that daemon/src/launcher.ts is present while a ticket-enumerated list would have omitted it. Then locate the current 'defect-class scope' standard text (shared standards and Oscar persona) and identify exactly where a 'at delegation time, re-derive the defect-class site set from the live tree (one grep) rather than trusting an enumerated ticket list' line would attach.

Mode 2 (loose first directive on multi-owner edit): Locate the synced base/template copy pair involved in run_181 Atom F and the cross-copy guard test that was weakened into a tautology. Show the file paths of all copies and the guard test path. Identify where in the Oscar persona / shared standards delegation guidance a 'when a behavior has multiple known owners, the directive must name ALL owners as mandatory (not as an aside)' line would attach.

Mode 3 (Oscar's own support edits not held to the evidence bar): Quote the existing shared-standards line added in commit e0ced3f about "'just docs' can still be behavior-pinned" and show its exact location. Identify the narrow residual edit that makes that rule explicitly cover Oscar's OWN Surface-A support edits (closing tickets, INDEX/Playbook/priority-doc edits), not only builder atoms. This is a one-line extension; show the before text and the proposed after text.

Mode 4 (unproven wrap / F18 runnable proof): Confirm by listing scripts/ that there is NO scripts/proof-*.mjs covering the run_181 D/E onboarding behavior that the wrap leaned on a manual founder reset for. Locate the wrap-up Play (packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md) and shared-standards text governing closeout, and identify where a wrap-content EXPECTATION attaches: a wrap resting on a manual founder gate must NAME its proof harness or a justified reason none is feasible. Confirm the framing must be require-justification (prompt/standard text), NEVER a wrap-blocking precondition that refuses (cite ADR-0029 founder-blocking anti-pattern and F5). 

Acceptance criteria: report covers all four modes with raw command output as evidence; every proposed change is drafted as text only (no files edited); no governance-of-governance checker is proposed; for each mode the owning file+anchor for the proposed text is named so a follow-up landing atom can apply it. Must not break: nothing is edited, so the tree stays clean.

data.reason: Read-only research atom produced no verifiable artifact: report lives only in Bob's ephemeral transcript (no bob.out capture, nothing in packages/** scope, clean tree) so the gate has no substance to check; my directive scoping error — research with no committable home.

issues: **Recommended Next Step** must be exactly Priority: `slug` — <focus> or Ticket: `NNNN` — <focus>
**Recommended Next Step** is out of order
**Recommended Next Step** must be exactly Priority: `slug` — <focus> or Ticket: `NNNN` — <focus>

data.summary: First occurrence of this exact wrap-up format fault: the headless wrap-up Play produced a founder closeout whose **Recommended Next Step** was out of order and did not match the required ready-work-item form, and the brief did not start with **Founder Completion Brief**. The CoCoder-owned contract is already mapped and pinned: packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md owns the closeout format, packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts parses that Play contract and validates output, the runner already made one repair attempt, and tests cover retry plus malformed wrap-up triage. The runner correctly blocked the malformed closeout instead of delivering it, so this is an isolated model/Play-output miss unless this fingerprint recurs.
data.fingerprint: wrapup-format-invalid|wrap-up play wrap-up produced malformed founder closeout: **recommended next step** is out of order; **founder completion brief** must be first; **recommended next step** must be exactly priority: slug — <focus> or ticket: nnnn — <focus>

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 2 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Nothing out of lane.
